description: Adversarial DQ rule patterns — rules designed by reasoning about failure modes, not just EDA observations. @dq-rule-writer must evaluate each pattern using the adversarial rule writing protocol.

patterns.description: No duplicate rows at the declared grain
patterns.sql_template: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT {grain_fields}, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM {table} GROUP BY {grain_fields} HAVING cnt > 1)

patterns.description: All FK references resolve to existing parent rows
patterns.sql_template: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {child_table} c LEFT JOIN {parent_table} p ON {join_condition} WHERE p.{parent_key} IS NULL

patterns.description: Start date must be before or equal to end date
All expected time periods are present per entity
patterns.sql_template: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT {entity_field}, COUNT(DISTINCT {period_field}) AS periods FROM {table} GROUP BY {entity_field} HAVING periods < {min_periods})

patterns.description: Values must be within expected range (from EDA)
Value variance must be non-zero (detect value spiking)
Cross-column business rules hold (domain-specific). Template reminds @dq-rule-writer to reason about what cross-column relationships must hold.
patterns.note: Domain project defines specific cross-column rules (e.g., total = sum of parts)
patterns.mandatory_when: table has derived or related columns
